title: lessingia cymulosa
intro: Lessingia cymulosa, also known as the sea gull plant or lesser sea lavender, is a perennial shrub that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Lessingia cymulosa include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and it is often used topically to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
